title: International Booker 2022 and Comparison
focus: Focuses on Geetanjali Shree's historic win and provides a direct comparison between the two Booker prizes for clarity.
rationale: Fallback section derived from packet-level grouping.
sectionExcerpt:
[Page 116]
The International Booker Prize is an international literary award hosted in the United Kingdom.
* The introduction of the International Prize to complement the Man Booker Prize, as the Booker Prize was then known, was announced in June 2004.
* Sponsored by the Man Group.
* Since 2016, the award has been given annually to a single work of fiction or collection of short stories, translated into English and published in the United Kingdom or Ireland, with a £50,000 prize for the winning title, shared equally between author and translator.
2022 Winner Details:
* Year: 2022
* Author: Geetanjali Shree
* Country: India
* Translator: Daisy Rockwell
* Translator Country: United States
* Work: Tomb of Sand (रेत समाधि)
* Original Language: Hindi

[Page 117]
Booker Prize vs. International Booker Prize. Booker Prize (formerly Man Booker Prize): Started: 1969. For Best Novel in English. Originally written in English. Authors of ANY Nationality. Published in UK/Ireland. Prize: £50,000 - Author Only. Indian Winners (Booker Prize): Arundhati Roy - "The God of Small Things" (1997); Aravind Adiga - "The White Tiger" (2008); Salman Rushdie - "Midnight's Children" (1981). International Booker Prize: Started: 2005 (Annual from 2016). For Best Translated Fiction. ANY Language, Translated to English. Author & Translator BOTH Win. Prize: £50,000 Split 50-50!. Indian Connection (International Booker Prize): Geetanjali Shree - "Tomb of Sand" (2022); Trans: Daisy Rockwell; 1st Hindi Novel to Win!. Quick Comparison: [Booker Prize: English, 1969, £50K - Only Author, Arundhati Roy] [Intl. Booker Prize: Translated, 2005, £50K - 50:50 Split (Author & Translator), Geetanjali Shree]. Summary: Booker Prize - Original English Novel; Intl. Booker Prize - Translated Fiction!
evidenceBullets: The International Booker Prize is an international literary award hosted in the United Kingdom.
* The introduction of the International Prize to complement the Man Booker Prize, as the Booker Prize was then known, was announced in June 2004.

* Since 2016, the award has been given annually to a single work of fiction or collection of short stories, translated into English and published in the United Kingdom or Ireland, with a £50,000 prize for the winning title, shared equally between author and translator.
